sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच
पार्थ नैवेह नामुत्र विनाशस्तस्य विद्यते |
न हि कल्याणकृत्कश्चिद्दुर्गतिं तात गच्छति ||
transliteration:
śrī-bhagavān uvāca
pārtha naiveha nāmutra vināśas tasya vidyate
na hi kalyāṇa-kṛt kaścid durgatiṁ tāta gacchati
literal_meaning: The Blessed Lord said: O son of Pṛthā, neither here nor there is destruction for him; for one who performs auspicious actions, dear friend, never meets with misfortune.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reassures Arjuna that sincere spiritual effort is never wasted; even partial progress on the path of inner alignment (yoga) protects the practitioner from ultimate failure or regression.
psychological_state: Post-setback rebound—recovering self-efficacy after perceived failure in meditation or lifestyle change.
trigger_scenario: You abandon a 30-day mindfulness app streak and feel you've 'lost everything'; this verse counters the all-or-nothing spiral.
anchor_text: Every sincere step on the path of goodness is eternally protected and never lost.
